title: World War I: military & diplomacy
description: Test your knowledge on the politics of World War I!
topic_path: World War I: military and diplomacy | 7.5

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ef5f9fbee6acd6b3db758e5b44a14d1.jpg)

>-Source: Oscar Cesare, “The American War-Dog,” [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_American_War-Dog_by_Oscar_Cesare_1916.jpg) 1916

**The image most directly illustrates that in 1916, United States foreign policy emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: remaining neutral in times of international conflict
containing the spread of communism in Asia
intervening on behalf of South American countries
supporting the Allies in Europe and entering the war

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ef5f9fbee6acd6b3db758e5b44a14d1.jpg)

>-Source: Oscar Cesare, “The American War-Dog,” [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_American_War-Dog_by_Oscar_Cesare_1916.jpg) 1916

**During the early twentieth century, politicians, such as the one depicted in the image, most likely would have opposed which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: involvement in an international conflict without a direct threat
trading goods with countries on opposing sides of a war
selling goods to a country in a war that the US was not directly involved in
refusing to join a war after American lives were lost in an act of military aggression

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ef5f9fbee6acd6b3db758e5b44a14d1.jpg)

>-Source: Oscar Cesare, “The American War-Dog,” [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_American_War-Dog_by_Oscar_Cesare_1916.jpg) 1916

**Which of the following statements best summarizes the main idea of the image about US involvement in World War I?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States government was following a foreign policy of isolationism and refused to intervene in World War I.
The United States was concerned about the fall of democratic governments in Europe and entered into World War I.
The American economy was suffering after a costly war with Spain and could not gather enough funds to enter into World War I.
The American public was against any aggressive military action, but the president declared war on Germany and entered World War I.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8151ceaf51846c91ad8ed06170c2d1d2.jpg)

>-Source: “Some Promise,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Some_Promise.jpg) 1917

**Which of the following events is this image most directly referencing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Zimmerman Telegram
the De Lome Letter
the sinking of the *Lusitania*
the explosion on the *USS Maine*

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8151ceaf51846c91ad8ed06170c2d1d2.jpg)

>-Source: “Some Promise,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Some_Promise.jpg) 1917

**The developments referenced by the image most directly contributed to United States involvement in which international conflict?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: World War I
Spanish-American War
World War II
Mexican-American War

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8151ceaf51846c91ad8ed06170c2d1d2.jpg)

>-Source: “Some Promise,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Some_Promise.jpg) 1917

**Which of the following developments is the most direct effect of the situation portrayed in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States declared war on Germany, entering World War I.
The United States declared war on Mexico, starting the Mexican-American War.
The United States ordered Germany to end unrestricted submarine warfare.
The United States signed a treaty with Mexico and agreed not to declare war on Germany.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2128ae8bc76a0ff8580bb8f6a755e50c.jpg)

> *Note: The three men sitting down are Senators Lodge, Borah, and Johnson. The woman standing up has the word peace written on her apron.*

>-Rollin Kirby, [Library of Congress](https://www.loc.gov/item/2016682645/),1919-1920

**During the 1920s, politicians, such as the ones depicted in the image, most likely would have opposed which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of peacetime alliances with other countries
the use of tariffs to encourage the sale of domestic goods
the policies to restrict immigration from war-torn Europe
the request for reparations from Germany

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2128ae8bc76a0ff8580bb8f6a755e50c.jpg)

>*Note: The three men sitting down are Senators Lodge, Borah, and Johnson. The woman standing up has the word peace written on her apron.*

>-Rollin Kirby, [Library of Congress](https://www.loc.gov/item/2016682645/),1919-1920

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the failure of the United States to join the League of Nations
the economic downturn following involvement in World War I
the end of the United States’ policy of isolationism
the defeat of the Nineteenth Amendment to reach ratification

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2128ae8bc76a0ff8580bb8f6a755e50c.jpg)

>*Note: The three men sitting down are Senators Lodge, Borah, and Johnson. The woman standing up has the word peace written on her apron.*

>-Rollin Kirby, [Library of Congress](https://www.loc.gov/item/2016682645/),1919-1920

**Which of the following best describes the message in the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: By refusing to ratify the Treaty of Versailles, Congress ensured that the world would not find peace in the aftermath of the war.
By refusing to join the League of Nations, Congress made sure that the United States would not enter into another World War.
By joining the League of Nations, Congress ensured that the United States would never know peace again and fight in another World War.
By ratifying the Treaty of Versailles, the United States solidified its status as a peacekeeping and policing world power.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a40d494a44d252646ebb60088524b7df.png)

>-Source: Leonard Raven-Hill, "The Gap in the Bridge," [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Gap_in_the_Bridge.png) 1919

**Which of the following statements best summarizes the message of the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States government’s failure to join the League of Nations has set the organization up to fail.
The United States government’s decision to stay out of World War I helped the economy flourish.
The United States’ leadership in the negotiation of the Treaty of Versailles solidified the US as a world power.
The United States’ involvement in World War I led to an influx of immigrants from European countries.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a40d494a44d252646ebb60088524b7df.png)

>-Source: Leonard Raven-Hill, "The Gap in the Bridge," [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Gap_in_the_Bridge.png) 1919

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the failed ratification of the Treaty of Versailles
the decision to join the League of Nations
the end of an isolationist foreign policy
the rise in national debt following the war

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a40d494a44d252646ebb60088524b7df.png)

>-Source: Leonard Raven-Hill, "The Gap in the Bridge," [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Gap_in_the_Bridge.png) 1919

**The image most directly illustrates a United States foreign policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: refusing to enter into any alliances with other countries while at peace
intervening on behalf of any Latin American country threatened by European powers
reducing restrictions for European immigrants from war-torn countries
supporting the development of democratic governments in European countries
